context.description: A philosophical book page argues that people retrofit explanations to preserve inner consistency and meaning. It compares neuroscience with mythology and biblical symbolism, then shifts toward the body as the next topic in understanding consciousness.
normalization_notes: Classified as nonfiction book body prose with theoretical discussion.

rows.paragraph_text: Self-narration is presented as a retrospective construction that adapts explanations to facts in order to preserve consistency. Neuroscience is compared with mythology and biblical symbolism as different languages for describing the same human condition, before the discussion turns to the body as a necessary next focus.

content.free_text: nonfiction_book_page book_body_content theoretical_discussion Philosophical nonfiction on self-narration, neuroscience, mythology, symbolism, and the body.
